worth_it_explanation: Audience setup on Yahoo DSP lines controls who the campaign reaches and how effectively the optimizer can find the right users. A line that bypasses the advertiser's own first-party segments in favor of broad category data is leaving the most precise signal unused. A composite audience stacked with too many AND conditions can reduce the addressable pool to the point where the optimizer lacks enough inventory to hit campaign goals. A retargeting segment with no recency filter keeps users from months-old visits in the pool alongside users who viewed a product page yesterday. None of these are structural rebuilds; each is a targeting configuration that can be adjusted at the line level.
intro: Audience setup determines which users a Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) line reaches and whether the targeting logic reflects the campaign goal. Yahoo DSP supports first-party audiences built from the Yahoo Dot pixel, customer data uploaded through Yahoo ConnectID, Yahoo's own curated data segments built from its owned-and-operated properties, and third-party data segments. These checks confirm that first-party audiences are applied where the advertiser has usable data, that composite audience logic does not narrow the addressable pool so far that delivery suffers, and that retargeting segments carry a recency window aligned with the actual consideration window for the product.

audit_checks.title: First-party audiences are not applied where the advertiser has usable data
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When an advertiser has a usable first-party audience from the Yahoo Dot pixel or an uploaded customer list and that audience is not applied on prospecting or retargeting lines, the targeting defaults to broad category data. The campaign then reaches users who fit a general profile rather than users with a demonstrated relationship to the brand, which reduces the relevance signal available to the optimizer.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP supports first-party audiences built from the Yahoo Dot pixel, which advertisers place on their own web properties to capture site-visitor data. Those pixel-based segments, along with customer lists uploaded through Yahoo ConnectID (the platform's identity solution), represent the advertiser's own data about their customers and prospects. Yahoo ConnectID enables identity matching at the user and household level across Yahoo's logged-in user base in the United States.

When an advertiser has a usable first-party audience and it is not applied to a line, the line targets users through Yahoo's curated data segments or third-party segments instead. Those segments describe users based on general interest categories or demographic attributes, which are less precise than signals derived from the advertiser's own customer interactions. The optimizer on the line works with the targeting it is given; if first-party data is available but not applied, the optimizer is working from a broader, less specific signal.

A few signs point to this situation:
  • Active first-party audiences appear in the Yahoo DSP Audiences section but are not assigned to any active line
  • Lines targeting product categories or interest segments for a brand that also runs a Yahoo Dot pixel on a high-traffic site
  • Retargeting lines that target Yahoo category segments rather than site-visitor audiences built from pixel data
You may want to confirm whether the advertiser's available first-party audiences are assigned to the lines where they are most relevant before concluding that the current audience setup is intentional.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a retail advertiser account, the most relevant first-party audiences are typically built from the Yahoo Dot pixel firing on product pages, the cart, and the purchase confirmation page. You may want to confirm that those pixel-based segments are applied on retargeting lines and that any customer lists uploaded through Yahoo ConnectID are in use on prospecting lines alongside suppression logic for recent purchasers.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation advertiser account, the most relevant first-party audiences are typically built from pixel data on high-intent pages such as the pricing page, the contact form, and the form submission confirmation. You may want to confirm that those site-visitor segments are applied on retargeting lines rather than relying entirely on Yahoo category data.

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative guidance on building and managing Yahoo Dot pixel audiences and ConnectID-based segments is available in the Yahoo DSP Help Center, which requires a Yahoo DSP login to access.

investigate.intro: Check the advertiser's available first-party audiences in Yahoo DSP and confirm whether those audiences are applied on active lines.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: In Yahoo DSP, navigate to the advertiser and open the Audiences section. Note which first-party audiences exist, including pixel-based site-visitor segments and any uploaded customer lists.
For each active line under the advertiser's campaigns, open the line's Targeting settings and review the Audience targeting section. Note which audiences are applied.

Cross-reference the available first-party audiences against the audiences applied on each line. Flag any line that targets only Yahoo curated or third-party segments when usable first-party audiences exist.
Note any first-party audiences with a size of zero or a very small member count, as those may indicate that the Yahoo Dot pixel is not firing correctly on the relevant pages.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line, whether any first-party audiences are applied and whether usable first-party audiences exist for the advertiser. A line that applies first-party audiences alongside Yahoo or third-party segments passes. A line that targets only broad category or demographic segments while usable first-party audiences exist in the account is a flag, recorded with the line name and the available first-party audiences that were not applied. A line with no relevant first-party audiences available is recorded as not applicable for this check.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the line name, the audiences applied, and the first-party audiences available in the account above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more active lines on {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP advertiser account target broad Yahoo curated or third-party segments while usable first-party audiences built from the Yahoo Dot pixel or uploaded customer data are available in the account but not applied. The optimizer on those lines works from a more general audience signal than the advertiser's own data would provide. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing which lines would benefit from first-party audience targeting and assigning the relevant pixel-based or uploaded segments, using Yahoo ConnectID-enabled audiences where available. Suppressing recent purchasers or converted leads on prospecting lines at the same time can improve the efficiency of both prospecting and retargeting spend.

audit_checks.title: Composite audience logic is stacked with AND conditions that collapse addressable reach
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Composite audiences in Yahoo DSP allow advertisers to combine segments with AND or OR logic. Stacking multiple independent AND conditions, which require a user to be in every segment simultaneously, can reduce the addressable pool to the point where delivery volume drops and the optimizer lacks sufficient inventory to pace or optimize effectively.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP supports composite audiences that combine multiple segments using boolean logic. An AND condition requires a user to qualify for every segment in the combination; an OR condition requires a user to qualify for at least one. Yahoo's own documentation for its composite audience feature notes that advertisers should use AND logic to fine-tune an audience by intersecting a first-party segment with another relevant audience, which produces a smaller but more precise pool.

The problem arises when AND logic is stacked across three or more independent conditions, each of which already narrows the pool significantly. A line that requires a user to simultaneously be a site visitor from the past 30 days AND in a Yahoo interest segment for a specific category AND within a particular geographic market AND on a specific device type may end up with an addressable pool too small for the optimizer to work with. The line may underpace, miss frequency targets, or deliver against whichever condition is loosest while the others effectively make no difference.

A few signs point to overly narrow AND stacking:
  • A composite audience applies three or more independent AND conditions, each of which covers a different targeting dimension
  • The line shows low delivery volume or consistent underpacing despite an adequate bid and budget
  • The estimated audience size shown in the line's targeting panel is small relative to the geographic market and product category
You may want to review whether each AND condition in the composite audience is genuinely required or whether some of the conditions could be relaxed to OR, or moved to separate lines with different budget allocations.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, composite audiences often combine a site-visitor segment with a product-category interest segment and a geographic filter. You may want to confirm that the combination still leaves an addressable pool large enough for the optimizer to work with, particularly for niche product categories where each individual segment is already small.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, composite audiences often intersect a job-title or industry segment with a geographic and a behavioral condition. You may want to confirm that the intersection is not so narrow that delivery is constrained, especially in markets outside major metropolitan areas where the segment population may be naturally small.

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative guidance on building composite audiences and using AND versus OR logic in Yahoo DSP is available in the Yahoo DSP Help Center, which requires a Yahoo DSP login to access.

investigate.intro: Review the composite audience configuration on active lines, noting the number of AND conditions and the resulting estimated audience size.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Open an active line and navigate to its Targeting settings. Locate the Audience targeting section.
Review the composite audience structure. Note how many segment conditions are joined with AND logic versus OR logic.
Count the number of independent AND conditions. A line with more than two independent AND conditions, each covering a different targeting dimension, warrants closer review.

Check the estimated audience size displayed in the targeting panel or the line's delivery forecast. A pool that appears very small relative to the campaign's geographic market and product category is a signal that the AND stacking may be too aggressive.
Note any lines showing consistent underpacing or low delivery volume in campaign reporting, which can indicate that the targeting is too restrictive for the optimizer to work with.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line using composite audiences, the number of independent AND conditions and the estimated audience size. A line with one or two AND conditions and an audience size proportionate to the campaign's geographic scope passes. A line with three or more independent AND conditions, or a line showing an audience size so small that it explains consistent underpacing, is a flag recorded with the line name, the number of AND conditions, and the estimated audience size.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the line name, the number of AND conditions in the composite audience, and the estimated audience size above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more active lines on {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP account apply composite audiences with AND conditions stacked across multiple independent targeting dimensions. The resulting addressable pools may be too small for the optimizer to pace or optimize effectively, which can explain any underpacing or low delivery volume on those lines. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the AND conditions in each composite audience and relaxing those that are not essential to the campaign goal, either by switching independent conditions to OR logic or by splitting the targeting into separate lines with distinct budget allocations. Reducing the number of simultaneous AND requirements gives the optimizer more inventory to work with while still reflecting the campaign's intent.

audit_checks.title: Retargeting segments lack a recency window, including stale site visitors in the audience
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A retargeting segment that accumulates users without a recency window includes visitors from months or years ago alongside recent visitors. The older users are unlikely to still be in the consideration phase, so impressions served to them have lower relevance than impressions served to recent site visitors. The overall quality of the retargeting pool declines as the proportion of stale users grows.

educate.base:
Retargeting segments in Yahoo DSP are built from the Yahoo Dot pixel, which fires when a user visits a page on the advertiser's site. Over time, a segment without a recency restriction accumulates users from progressively older visits. A user who visited a product page 180 days ago and a user who visited the same page yesterday are both in the segment, but their likelihood of being in the active consideration phase for a purchase is very different.

A recency window limits the segment to users whose qualifying pixel fire occurred within a defined number of days. Setting a recency window keeps the segment focused on users who are more likely to still be considering a purchase, and reduces wasted impressions on users who have long since made a decision or lost interest.

The appropriate recency window depends on the product category's typical consideration period. A consumable with a short repurchase cycle may suit a 7- to 14-day window. A high-consideration product, such as consumer electronics or home furnishings, may suit a 30- to 90-day window. An enterprise software or professional-services brand may warrant a window of 90 days or longer. Setting the window in Yahoo DSP is done at the audience configuration level for pixel-based segments.

A few signs point to a missing or misaligned recency window:
  • A retargeting segment shows a very large member count relative to the site's typical monthly visitor volume, suggesting historical accumulation without expiry
  • No recency setting is documented in the audience configuration for the segment
  • Retargeting campaign frequency reports show high repeat impression counts against a pool much larger than recent site-visit numbers would suggest
You may want to review the recency configuration for each retargeting segment attached to active lines and confirm that the window reflects the product category's actual consideration period.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a retail advertiser account, cart-abandonment and product-page visitor segments typically benefit from a short recency window. A 7- to 30-day window is a reasonable starting point for most retail categories, where the gap between a product-page visit and a purchase decision is typically short. You may want to confirm that older visitors are not being carried in the retargeting pool alongside recent cart abandoners, where the two groups likely have very different purchase intent.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation advertiser account, the consideration period for high-stakes services such as legal, financial, or professional services can extend well beyond 30 days. A recency window that is too short may exclude prospects still actively evaluating options. You may want to confirm that the window reflects the actual time between a first site visit and a form submission for the advertiser's specific service category.

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative guidance on configuring recency windows for Yahoo Dot pixel retargeting segments is available in the Yahoo DSP Help Center, which requires a Yahoo DSP login to access.

investigate.intro: Review the recency configuration for each retargeting segment attached to active lines, and cross-reference segment sizes against the site's typical visitor volume.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: In Yahoo DSP, navigate to the Audiences section for the advertiser. For each pixel-based retargeting segment attached to an active line, check whether a recency window is configured and note the duration.

Compare the segment's current size against the site's typical monthly visitor volume for the pages that trigger the pixel. A segment substantially larger than a few months of expected visits may indicate historical accumulation without a recency limit.

Open each active retargeting line and confirm which segment is applied. Note any line where the segment has no recency window or where the window duration seems longer than the product category's typical consideration period.

In campaign reporting, check frequency metrics for each retargeting line. A high average frequency against a very large audience pool can indicate that the retargeting segment is catching stale users who are not converting but consuming impressions.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per retargeting segment on active lines, whether a recency window is configured and the duration. A segment with a recency window proportionate to the product category's typical consideration period passes. A segment with no recency window, or a window that is longer than what the consideration period warrants, is a flag recorded with the segment name, the current window setting, and the segment size relative to expected site-visitor volume. A very large segment with no window is recorded as a priority flag.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the segment name, the recency window setting, and the segment size for each retargeting segment on active lines above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more retargeting segments attached to active lines on {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP account do not have a recency window configured, or have a window that extends well beyond the likely consideration period for the product category. Without a recency limit, the segments accumulate site visitors from an extended period, reducing the proportion of the audience still actively considering a purchase. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the recency configuration for each retargeting segment and setting a window that reflects the typical time between a first site visit and a conversion for each product category. For most retail categories, a 14- to 30-day window is a reasonable starting point; for higher-consideration products, a 60- to 90-day window may be more appropriate.
